"Stevenson's visit was so stealthy
that few people knew just how long
he was here. Mr.. Stevenson is a pro-
fessional railroad Lobbyist. He is the
presiding genius Of Temple Flat, the
famous railroad 'hangout' in Olympia
which Governor Mcbride has so Wig
orously denounced in his speeches and
interviews. outside Of a few fish
wheels which he owns on the Column
bia river Mr.. Stevenson has no other
bushes save that Of political agent
and LOBBYIST for the railroads. He is
the special representative Of the Har-
riman interests. "-Spokesman-Rev
of Nov. 8, 1902.


AS to Mr.. Rockefeller:


1.-THE people believe that the Stand
ard OIL is the greatest monopoly in
existence; that Is Is a trust Of
the worst form, which should not be
permitted to engage in interstate
commerce without an effort on the
part of the federal government to put
an end to further extortation."-Spok
esman-Review, Feb. I, 1908.


1.-THE Wall street correspondent of
the Spokesman Review says, that 1t
is reliably reported there that the
Rockefeller Standard Oil interests
have increased their representation in
the management Of the Northwestern
and the Atchison roads; that they
now control the Union Pacific and ex-
pect to get the Northern Pacific even


ually. thus largely debating western
| railroad policy" = The Spokesman


Review of Oct. IA. 1904.


AS to Mr.. Cowles:


1.-THE truthful Spokesman-Review


announced In large type on Tuesday:
| Two thousand people hear Turner


in Seattle" The hall holds one thous-


and and wasn't full by any means.
The Review also said that the people


stood up in scores all over the house.
| waving their hats and cheering" when
Mr. Turner was introduced. This. too


will be news. even to the Democrats
who were present. A Spokesman-Re-
view report of any campaign matter
| must always be discounted so to 75
per cent. before II can be taken with


safety. The acknowledgement. Oct.
IS, 1904.


AS to Mr.. Turner:


| Senator Turner admitted that he
would be general counsel for the


new road. 1t is stated that Senator
| Turner is now chief legal adviser for
| the Canadian Pacific in this section of
the United States. "-Spokesman-Re
| view, Dec. "a. 1902.


MANY people believe that Senator
